title: PHY: SF orthogonality for parallel channels
description:
## Problem

Different LoRa spreading factors are quasi-orthogonal. SF7 and SF12 transmissions can overlap without collision. This is effectively 6 parallel channels on the same frequency.

## Hardware requirement

**Works on ALL hardware.** Any LoRa chip supports SF7-SF12.

## Solution

Assign different SFs to different node groups:
1. Gateway listens on all SFs (or round-robin)
2. Nodes are assigned an SF (by gateway or hash of IID)
3. Nodes TX/RX only on assigned SF
4. Cross-SF communication goes through gateway

## Assignment methods

### Hash-based
  node_sf = SF7 + (hash(iid) mod 6)
- Simple, no coordination
- Peers on different SFs can't direct-communicate

### Gateway-assigned
- Gateway assigns SF in DIO or join response
- Load balancing: put new nodes on least-loaded SF
- Dynamic rebalancing

### Topology-aware
- Core nodes (high degree): SF7 (fast, short range)
- Edge nodes (low degree): SF12 (slow, long range)

## Tradeoffs

- Cross-SF peers need gateway relay (no direct P2P)
- Gateway must handle all SFs
- But: 6x capacity on same frequency

## Acceptance criteria

- Spec defines SF assignment method
- Gateway implements multi-SF RX
- Nodes honor assigned SF
- Scale test shows reduced collision rate
